heading: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis registry
label: 280g–7 Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis registry

children.label: A
children.text: develop a system to collect data on amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (referred to in this section as “ALS”) and other motor neuron disorders that can be confused with ALS, misdiagnosed as ALS, and in some cases progress to ALS, including information with respect to the incidence and prevalence of the disease in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: establish a national registry for the collection and storage of such data to develop a population-based registry of cases in the United States of ALS and other motor neuron disorders that can be confused with ALS, misdiagnosed as ALS, and in some cases progress to ALS.

children.label: A
children.text: better describe the incidence and prevalence of ALS in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: examine appropriate factors, such as environmental and occupational, that may be associated with the disease;

children.label: C
children.text: better outline key demographic factors (such as age, race or ethnicity, gender, and family history of individuals who are diagnosed with the disease) associated with the disease;

children.label: D
children.text: better examine the connection between ALS and other motor neuron disorders that can be confused with ALS, misdiagnosed as ALS, and in some cases progress to ALS; and

children.label: E
children.text: other matters as recommended by the Advisory Committee established under subsection (b).

children.label: I
children.text: the National Institutes of Health, to include, upon the recommendation of the Director of the National Institutes of Health, representatives from the National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke and the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences;

children.label: II
children.text: the Department of Veterans Affairs;

children.label: III
children.text: the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention; and

children.label: ii
children.text: of which at least one such member shall be a clinician with expertise on ALS and related diseases, an epidemiologist with experience in data registries, a statistician, an ethicist, and a privacy expert (relating to the privacy regulations under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996); and

children.label: i
children.text: national and voluntary health associations;1So in original. Probably should be “national voluntary health associations;”.1

children.label: ii
children.text: patients with ALS or their family members;

children.label: iii
children.text: clinicians with expertise on ALS and related diseases;

children.label: iv
children.text: epidemiologists with experience in data registries;

children.label: v
children.text: geneticists or experts in genetics who have experience with the genetics of ALS or other neurological diseases and2So in original. Probably should be followed by a semicolon.2

children.label: vi
children.text: other individuals with an interest in developing and maintaining the National ALS Registry.

children.label: A
children.text: the development and maintenance of the National ALS Registry;

children.label: B
children.text: the type of information to be collected and stored in the Registry;

children.label: C
children.text: the manner in which such data is to be collected;

children.label: D
children.text: the use and availability of such data including guidelines for such use; and

children.label: E
children.text: the collection of information about diseases and disorders that primarily affect motor neurons that are considered essential to furthering the study and cure of ALS.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than 270 days after the date on which the Advisory Committee is established, the Advisory Committee may submit a report to the Secretary concerning the review conducted under paragraph (2) that contains the recommendations of the Advisory Committee with respect to the results of such review.

children.heading: Grants
children.label: c Grants
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, may award grants to, and enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with, public or private nonprofit entities for the collection, analysis, and reporting of data on ALS and other motor neuron disorders that can be confused with ALS, misdiagnosed as ALS, and in some cases progress to ALS after receiving the report under subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: Coordination with State, local, and Federal registries
children.label: d Coordination with State, local, and Federal registries

children.heading: In general3So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.3
children.label: 1 In general3So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.3
i
children.text: any registry pilot projects previously supported by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Department of Veterans Affairs ALS Registry;

children.label: iii
children.text: the DNA and Cell Line Repository of the National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke Human Genetics Resource Center at the National Institutes of Health;

children.label: iv
children.text: Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry studies, including studies conducted in Illinois, Missouri, El Paso and San Antonio, Texas, and Massachusetts;

children.label: v
children.text: State-based ALS registries;

children.label: vi
children.text: the National Vital Statistics System; and

children.label: vii
children.text: any other existing or relevant databases that collect or maintain information on those motor neuron diseases recommended by the Advisory Committee established in subsection (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: provide for research access to ALS data as recommended by the Advisory Committee established in subsection (b) to the extent permitted by applicable statutes and regulations and in a manner that protects personal privacy consistent with applicable privacy statutes and regulations.

children.heading: .—Coordination with nih and department of veterans affairs
children.label: C .—Coordination with nih and department of veterans affairs
children.text: Consistent with applicable privacy statutes and regulations, the Secretary may ensure that epidemiological and other types of information obtained under subsection (a) is made available to the National Institutes of Health and the Department of Veterans Affairs.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: e Definition
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the term “national voluntary health association” means a national non-profit organization with chapters or other affiliated organizations in States throughout the United States with experience serving the population of individuals with ALS and have demonstrated experience in ALS research, care, and patient services.
